_readme: This is YOUR inventory — replace every placeholder below with the real tools
you use and the real actions your business needs an agent to take. There is NO
built-in platform list; the map is only as true as what you write here.
For each action set:
tool    : the tool/platform the action lives in (your ad platform, your CRM, etc.)
action  : the DO verb the agent needs to perform ('publish the post', 'change the budget')
status  : 'green'  = the agent can perform this end-to-end (a write verb is exposed)
'yellow' = the agent can only READ it; a human still acts
'red'    = the action is not exposed to an agent at all (the wall)
reason  : for RED only — why it's red (drives which bridge the map recommends):
Bridge 1 (write lane exists, just not wired):
'write_scope_not_enabled'  - connector has a write tier you haven't turned on
'read_only_connector'      - current connector is read-only; a write tool exists
'has_api_not_wired'        - platform has an API for this; agent isn't calling it yet
'needs_automation_hub'     - route it through a no-code automation hub
Bridge 2 (can't / shouldn't be a machine write):
'no_api_for_action'        - no API path for this action at all
'compliance_block'         - policy requires a human in the loop
'too_risky_to_automate'    - spends money / deletes data; keep a human tap
note    : (optional) anything you want on the map for this action
If you're unsure of a status: have your agent test the READ and the WRITE for that
tool and report which actually goes through. A connector that 'connects' can still
be read-only. Tag by whether a write succeeds, not by whether it connects.

actions.note: spends client money — stage for approval
connector has a write tier we haven't enabled
API exists; give the agent a scoped script

actions.action: Write a status back to the entry
actions.note: no write API — stage a one-click approval
route through a no-code hub the agent triggers
